# Grain

> Infrastructure for the offline web. Deploy like infrastructure. Adopt like a framework.

Grain is an offline-first CSS + JS UI framework built for schools, NGOs, and developers who need UI that runs without internet. All classes use the `gr-` prefix. Self-hosted fonts included. Zero external dependencies.

## Core Files

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Complete AI coding instructions — class reference, patterns, rules
- [grain.css](dist/grain.css): Full framework stylesheet (modern browsers)
- [grain-lite.css](dist/grain-lite.css): Lite stylesheet (old browsers, no animations)
- [grain.js](dist/grain.js): Interactive components (tabs, modal, toast, dropdown)
- [grain-lite.js](dist/grain-lite.js): Lite JS (tabs, modal, dropdown only)
- [README.md](README.md): Installation and quick start
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): Version history

## Documentation

- [index.html](docs/index.html): Living documentation — all components with examples
- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): Complete token-efficient reference for AI code generation

## Key Concepts

- All CSS classes are prefixed `gr-` (e.g. `gr-btn`, `gr-grid`, `gr-frame`)
- JS data attributes use `data-grain-*` prefix
- JS global: `window.Grain`
- Two distribution modes: full (`grain.css`) and lite (`grain-lite.css`)
- Fonts self-hosted in `/fonts/` — no Google Fonts, no CDN
- Color tokens: `--gr-bg`, `--gr-ink`, `--gr-accent`, `--gr-accent2`, `--gr-accent3`
- Primary accent: burnt sienna `#b85c38`
- Zero border-radius by design

## Use Cases

- School intranets on local servers
- NGO field dashboards on mesh networks
- USB-deployable web tools
- Developer portfolios with terminal aesthetic
- Any web project requiring offline-first, dependency-free UI
